Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Fleet Agent sidecar — polling, dispatch, result streaming (#17) (#29)

* feat: add sidecar endpoints — pending tasks polling and event ingestion

Implements the fleet-api server side of Issue #17 (Fleet Agent sidecar):

- GET /agents/{agent_id}/tasks/pending — returns accepted tasks for the
  authenticated agent, ordered by priority DESC then created_at ASC.
  Agent can only poll their own tasks (403 on mismatch).

- POST /tasks/{task_id}/events — receives execution events from the
  sidecar (status, progress, log, completed, failed, heartbeat). Updates
  task status/progress/result via state machine. Validates monotonically
  increasing sequence numbers (422 on out-of-order). Only the task's
  executor agent can post events (403 on mismatch).

32 new tests across 3 files covering happy paths, authorization,
error cases, priority ordering, state transitions, and response format.
399 total tests passing, zero regressions.

Closes #17

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fleet agent sidecar package (Issue #17)

Implements the fleet_agent client sidecar for distributed task execution:
- poller.py: polls fleet-api for pending tasks with Ed25519 signed requests
  and exponential backoff on connection failure
- executor.py: dispatches tasks to local handler via async subprocess,
  parses newline-delimited JSON events from stdout, enforces timeouts
- streamer.py: streams task events back to fleet-api with auto-sequencing
  and retry on transient failures (connection errors, 5xx)
- health.py: GET /fleet/health endpoint reporting poller status,
  fleet-api reachability, and active task count
- config.py: pydantic-settings configuration from environment variables
- models.py: PendingTask and TaskEvent data models
- signing.py: Ed25519 request signing matching fleet-api auth middleware
- __main__.py: entry point (python -m fleet_agent)
- Dockerfile.sidecar and fleet-agent.service for deployment
- 33 tests covering all modules

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add HealthStatus model to models.py for typed health responses
- Update health endpoint to return HealthStatus instead of raw dict
- Add Ed25519PrivateKey type guard in __main__._load_private_key
- Simplify executor timeout handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hatchling build requires the complete src/ tree, not just fleet_agent.

commit.message:
Fix sentence checker rejecting valid morpheme-verb sentences

The hardcoded verbHints list didn't include any morpheme-derived words,
so sentences like "She disrupted the class." or "We import goods from
China." were incorrectly rejected — even when pasting the example sentence.

Fix: the target word itself now satisfies the verb check, since morpheme-
built words (import, disrupt, inspect, construct, transport, etc.) are
almost always the main verb of the sentence. The verbHints list is also
expanded with common auxiliary and action verbs for cases where the built
word is a noun form (transportation, inspection) paired with another verb.

This bug affected all 18 Latin root matrices and the Greek form matrices.

refactor(design-system): visual consistency Group 3 — icons, activeOpacity, handle bars, modal pattern

Fix #14 — Migrate all Ionicons to Lucide (except brand logos):
- app/_layout.tsx: alert-circle-outline→AlertCircle, bicycle-outline→Bike, close-outline→X
- app/(tabs)/discover/index.tsx: search→Search, shield-checkmark→ShieldCheck, chevron-forward→ChevronRight, compass-outline→Compass, arrow-forward→ArrowRight; bonus Group 1 color tokens (#F3F4F6, #111827, #6B7280, #9CA3AF)
- app/(tabs)/favs/index.tsx: 10 icons replaced (Utensils, FileText, ChevronRight, Pencil, Bike, ChevronLeft, Clipboard, CheckCircle2)
- components/ServingStepper.tsx: trash-outline→Trash2
- components/RepeatDaySheet.tsx: calendar-outline→CalendarDays
- components/RepeatWeekSheet.tsx: calendar-outline→CalendarDays
- components/WeeklyPlanView.tsx: time-outline→Clock, bicycle→Bike, calendar-outline→CalendarDays
- components/DailyPlanView.tsx: add-circle-outline→PlusCircle, bicycle-outline→Bike
- components/MealPickerSheet.tsx: 10+ icons replaced; Heart uses fill prop for conditional filled/outline state
- components/VoiceRecordSheet.tsx: stop→Square (fill=white for solid look), mic→Mic

Fix #15 — Standardise activeOpacity → 0.8 across all components:
- All non-standard values (0.7, 0.75, 0.82, 0.85) normalised to 0.8
- Intentional activeOpacity={1} values preserved (DateCircle, disabled states)
- Files: DailyPlanView, WeeklyPlanView, MealPickerSheet, VoiceRecordSheet, RepeatDaySheet, SegmentedControl, SlotPickerModal, favs/index

Fix #16 — Standardise bottom-sheet handle bar (w:36 h:4 r:99 mt:12 mb:4 bg:Colors.border):
- RepeatDaySheet, RepeatWeekSheet: width 40→36, borderRadius 2→99
- MealPickerSheet: borderRadius 2→99, marginTop 10→12
- VoiceRecordSheet: width 32→36, borderRadius 2→99, marginTop 16→12, added marginBottom 4

Fix #17 — Convert VoiceRecordSheet from transparent+overlay to pageSheet:
- Modal: transparent removed, presentationStyle="pageSheet" added
- Removed TouchableWithoutFeedback overlay backdrop
- Removed absolute positioning; sheet is now flex:1
- Removed useSafeAreaInsets (pageSheet handles safe area natively)
- Removed Shadows.card (not needed on native sheet)

feat(showcase): Level 1 "The Courtyard" — playable with puzzles, combat, and artifacts

Complete Level 1 for "Echoes of the Ancients" showcase game:
- Push-block puzzle (2 blocks, 2 pressure plates, 1 gate)
- 2 guardian enemies with patrol/chase AI
- Destructible wall hiding the artifact
- Fog, directional shadows, 4 point lights (torches)
- Updated game.lua with level cleanup, level names, completion flow
- HUD prompt fade fix (dt-based instead of ffe.after timer)
- Assets: cube.glb, PressStart2P TTF, placeholder audio (music, SFX)

feat: improve endpoint eval matching with compound expansion + token overlap

Gold safety endpoints are often compound ("liver function, coagulation,
platelets"). Expand these so each sub-item matches independently, and
absorb multiple extracted items matching the same compound gold entry.
Also add token overlap fallback (0.60) for abbreviated/paraphrased names.

Eval-only improvement: P=42.3%→62.5%, R=73.3%→83.3%, F1=53.7%→71.4%

fix(build): make vcpkg overlay ports conditional on MinGW target (macOS CI fix)

The LuaJIT overlay port was unconditionally shadowing the upstream vcpkg port
for all platforms. On macOS arm64, this caused build failures because the overlay
was designed only for Windows/MinGW cross-compilation. Now VCPKG_OVERLAY_PORTS
is only set when VCPKG_TARGET_TRIPLET matches *mingw*.

commit.message:
fix: use competitor name (not handle) for page search

fbPageName like "elartedelaquietud" (no spaces) returns wrong pages
with 0 likes on SearchAPI. Now always uses competitor.name for the
page search, and scores results by name similarity + likes to pick
the best match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Place new panels inside visible viewport with block-stacking layout

Rewrite findEmptyPosition to be viewport-aware:
- Read current viewport from ReactFlow instance to know visible area
- Strategy 1: snap to edges of existing nodes (right/bottom aligned)
  for a tidy block-stacking layout
- Strategy 2: grid scan within viewport for free slots
- Strategy 3: fall back to right of visible area
- Score candidates by in-viewport first, then proximity to top-left

Previously panels appeared at arbitrary flow coordinates with no
awareness of what the user could actually see.

commit.message:
fix(chat-bot): use hierarchy for team access, not roles

Team bot access is now determined by the agent's position in the
hierarchy tree — if any upline has a billing-exempt bot, the downline
agent qualifies. Replaces the incorrect role-based check (imo_owner,
imo_admin) which would have granted access to all role holders globally.

Also adds docs/standard-chat-bot-billing-exempt.md with the API
contract for the external standard-chat-bot project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: isolate security tests from Redis by mocking _webhook_cache in fixture

The lifespan startup overwrites _webhook_cache with the real CacheService
singleton after any pre-startup monkeypatching. Tests 1 and 2 were hitting
real Redis, which could set dedup/lock keys visible to test 3.

Fix: apply a null mock_cache both before lifespan (pre-emptive) and after
(re-apply post lifespan), ensuring all three security tests are fully
isolated from Redis state. Pattern mirrors test_admin_bot_training.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: multi-session concurrent streaming (#390)

* feat: extract per-conversation streaming state into streamStore

Move all streaming state (isStreaming, streamingContent, streamingReasoning,
streamingToolCalls, streamingParts, abortController, isCompressing,
compressionNotice) from chatStore into a new streamStore keyed by
conversationId. This enables multiple conversations to stream simultaneously.

- New streamStore with Record<string, ConversationStreamState>
- New useChatStreamState hook for components to read active stream state
- chatStore delegates streaming lifecycle to streamStore
- Guard message appending: only update chatStore.messages if the completed
  stream's conversationId matches the active conversation
- Abort stream on conversation delete (dataStore.deleteConversation)
- Sidebar pulsing dot indicator for actively streaming conversations
- Update all component consumers (MessageList, ChatInput, AssistantMessage)
- Update all existing tests, add streamStore and hook tests

Related to #389

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: per-conversation command cancellation and correct post-conversation messages

Two fixes from code review:

1. runPostConversationTasks now loads messages from DB via
   messageRepo.getByConversation instead of reading get().messages,
   which could belong to a different conversation after switching.

2. stopGeneration now uses cancelCommandsForConversation(activeId)
   instead of the global cancelAllActiveCommands(), so stopping one
   conversation does not kill bash commands running in others.
   The bash tool tracks conversationId per cancel token via Map.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: bind bash tool to conversation via factory to prevent cross-session leaks

Convert bashTool singleton to createBashTool(conversationId) factory so
each stream gets a bash instance bound to the correct conversation.
This prevents permission prompts and cancel tokens from being attributed
to the wrong conversation when the user switches tabs during streaming.

Thread conversationId through getAgentTools -> chat-stream-runner ->
chatStore for the full call chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: combine same-thread unread messages with timestamps for classifier

When multiple unread messages exist in the same Gmail thread, they are
merged into a single email text with date markers before classification.
This lets the classifier see the full picture (e.g. order + address)
and distinguish between old and new content by timestamps.

Extra messages are marked as "merged" in email_history to prevent
re-processing on subsequent triggers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

seo: add hreflang ar + x-default for geo-targeting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add axis reduction, size-1 broadcasting, where, stop_gradient

Four features needed for ML experiment porting:
- sum(x, axis) / mean(x, axis): reduce along specific dimension
- Size-1 broadcasting: f32[3,1] * f32[3,4] -> f32[3,4]
- where(cond, x, y): element-wise array conditional with AD
- stop_gradient(expr): identity forward, blocks gradient backward

All fully differentiable. Enables softmax, layer norm, attention,
RL training patterns (TD targets), and array masking.
